topic: Agentic AI for reliable academic literature review and hallucination mitigation in large language models
final_review:
The integration of Agentic AI in academic literature review has the potential to enhance the reliability and efficiency of the process (Kasneci et al., 2023). Explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) can be employed to provide insights into the decision-making process of AI models (Barredo Arrieta et al., 2019). XAI can help identify the sources of errors and enable the development of more transparent and trustworthy AI systems.

The use of Agentic AI in academic literature review can facilitate the analysis of complex datasets and identification of patterns that may not be apparent to human reviewers (Dwivedi et al., 2019). AI-powered tools can assist in the evaluation of the quality and relevance of academic sources, reducing the risk of including biased or inaccurate information in the review (Zawacki‐Richter et al., 2019). It is essential to ensure that AI models are designed and trained to prioritize accuracy and reliability (Dwivedi et al., 2023).

The development of Agentic AI for academic literature review requires a multidisciplinary approach, incorporating insights from education, computer science, and other relevant fields (Kasneci et al., 2023). By leveraging the capabilities of AI and addressing challenges associated with bias, it is possible to create more efficient and effective academic literature review processes (Dwivedi et al., 2023). The application of Agentic AI can lead to improved outcomes, including enhanced review quality and reduced reviewer workload (Dwivedi et al., 2019).

References:
Barredo Arrieta, A., Díaz-Rodríguez, N., Del Ser, J., 2019
Dwivedi, Y. K., Hughes, L., Ismagilova, E., 2019
Dwivedi, Y. K., Kshetri, N., Hughes, L., 2023
Kasneci, E., Seßler, K., Küchemann, S., 2023
